profile.note: Bench-side reading and a stack on the nightstand. Genres run Southwestern mystery, true crime, and American West history.

book.title: The Dust Road Killings
book.author: R. Halloran
book.notes: Reread. The detective character reads ground the way you would on a trail. Holds up.

book.title: Red Mesa Cold Case
book.author: D. Yazzie
book.notes: Half through. Procedure feels right, the dialogue less so.

book.title: The Long Stakeout
book.author: M. Prentice
book.notes: Patience as method. The twelve-hour-stakeout chapters land.

book.title: Evidence in the Caliche
book.author: L. Becenti
book.notes: Recommended by a colleague. On deck after the Red Mesa one.

book.title: Silver, Stone, and the Old Bench
book.author: T. Kee
book.notes: Mentor's recommendation. The chapter on overlay technique sent me back to the bench.

book.title: High Desert Trails of the Zuni Range
book.author: P. Whitehorse
book.notes: Reads like the country I walk. Good on terrain, light on people.

book.title: Bezel Setting and Stone Work
book.author: A. Ruiz
book.notes: Bench reference. Stays in the shop, not the nightstand.

book.title: The Window Rock Vanishing
book.author: R. Halloran
book.notes: Same author as RL-001. Saving it for a slow week.
